text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy town called Willow Creek, there was a kid named Charlie. Charlie's favorite thing in the world was ants. Not just any ants, but gigantic juicy black velvet ant spiders. He dreamt of building an outdoor stage just for them. One summer day, Charlie gathered every penny he had saved from lawn mowing and cleaned his grandmother’s house to buy some exotic plants.

He planted fangs of forest velvet, spikes of obsidian fern, and tendrils of coralhorn moss. Then he dug a gigantic pit with a roof made of spider webs. Charlie named it the Velvet Stage. Late nights, he watched them dance. His friends nicknamed him Ziggy.

One autumn, a fierce storm hit. Leaves blew fiercely, a giant whirlwind grew. Charlie looked up and saw branches coming down like swords. When it was over, he saw treasures beneath the roots—a book filled with magic spells, an ancient coin, and finally, his dream—a beautiful feather from an
